bad.body:
## Understanding Hacker Types

## Common Attack Types

repeat.line: Background taxonomy material with no executable guidance.

medium.body:
## When to Use

1. Review the supplied artifact.
medium.tail: - Adapted material; verify local paths, tools, credentials, and agent features before acting.

good.description: Review, validate, and audit README, API documentation, and changelog changes; use this skill when the user asks to check documentation before release, but do not use it for source-code review.
good.body:
## When to Use

1. Inspect the input.
2. Validate the output.
3. Report evidence.

```sh
check-one
```

```sh
check-two
```

If validation fails, fix it and re-run the checks before completion.

simple-skill.description: Rewrite the previous answer more briefly when the user asks for a concise version.
simple-skill.body:
## When to Use

Rewrite only the previous answer.

## Workflow

1. Preserve the substance.
2. Return a shorter answer.

## Limitations

Do not perform unrelated work.

instruction-only.description: Follow a bounded instruction-only workflow when the user asks to normalize one text response.
instruction-only.body:
## When to Use

Use only for the supplied text.

## Workflow

1. Inspect the text.
2. Normalize it.
3. Verify the output.

## Limitations

Do not use tools or external sources.

risky-workflow-without-feedback-loop.description: Run an authorized security review when the user explicitly supplies a permitted target.
risky-workflow-without-feedback-loop.body:
## When to Use

Use only with written authorization.

## Workflow

1. Run the approved database operation against the authorized target.
2. Report the finding.

## Limitations

Never act outside the approved scope.

progressive-disclosure-with-real-bundle.description: Design API pagination when the user asks for cursor or offset contract guidance.
progressive-disclosure-with-real-bundle.body:
## When to Use

Use for API pagination contract design.

## Workflow

1. Inspect the API constraints.
2. Validate the proposed contract.
3. Open `references/pagination.md` only for advanced pagination cases.

## Limitations

Do not implement unrelated backend code.

reference.text:
# Advanced pagination

Use cursor validation only for advanced pagination cases.
